profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:09:46 UTC.

ead.archdesc:
======= RMA02825Daniel G. Sisler papers21-1-2825Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
October 20172B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
agricThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:15:06 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Daniel G. Sisler papers
Sisler, Daniel Gene.
21-1-2825
4.1 cubic feet.
1929-1995 Correspondence, publications, awards, course materials, and biographical materials. Correspondence, subject files, clippings, photographs, honors and awards, memorabilia, academic writing, and other papers pertaining to the personal life and professional career of Daniel Sisler. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 5 ======= 5 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2713098
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Deborah Cooper
Date completed:
October 2017
EAD encoding:
Marcie Farwell, October 2017
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, October 2017
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Agricultural Resources and Managerial Economics, New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences at Cornell University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, subject files, clippings, photographs, honors and awards, memorabilia, academic writing, and other papers pertaining to the personal life and professional career of Daniel Sisler.
RELATED MATERIALS
Related collections: Carol Sisler papers, #4925, http://resolver.library.cornell.edu/cgi-bin/EADresolver?id=RMM04925.
Cite As:
Daniel G. Sisler papers, #21-1-2825. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Agricultural economics -- Study and teaching. Agricultural resources -- Study and teaching. Agriculture -- Study and teaching. Cornell University -- : Faculty. New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences -- : Faculty.

Includes correspondence with Veterans Admin about disability/blindness, and graduate agricultural studies.Cornell and Purdue1961-199019

Includes 1982 front page article and 1989 commemorative issue recounting 1969 student dissent.Purdue Braille NotebookUndated1

Looseleaf notebook in binder with Braille graphs produced by Dr. Sisler and used to learn fundamental, economic principles.Teaching and advisingCourse Outlines, materials and examsFirst Prelim Ag Ec 100, 1962-19921962-199221
Scope and Contents
Includes prelims and notes for Ag Ec 50, 100, 150.Final Exams 1965-19921965-199222
Scope and Contents
Final exams for Ag Ec 100, Ag Ec 150.Ag Ec 150 - Great Plains Wheat Master198123Ag Ec 150 - The Corn Belt Master198124Ag Ec 150 - Cotton Belt Master198425Ag Ec 150 - The Northeast Master1981-198426Ag Ec 150 - Rural NY State's Ag Districts Master198127Ag Ec 150 - Ag of the Empire Master198128Ag Ec 150 - Regions of the US MasterUndated29Ag Ec 150 - California Grape Growers Story Master1981210Ag Ec 150 - Cesar Chavez Master1981211Ag Ec 150 - Grazing and Irrigated Crops Crops Master1981212Ag Ec 150 - Pacific Coast Master1981213Ag Ec 150 - Agriculture in the Communist Giants1981214Ag Ec 100 - Full Year Sample Lectures (1)1988215Ag Ec 100 - Full Year Sample Lectures (2)1988216Ag Ec 100 Course Syllabus and Materials1993-1994219

Scope and Contents
Includes syllabus and "Take Note" lecture notes for each week of semester.

Was removed from original binder.Introduction to Global Issues of Agricultural EconomicsUndated2

Prof. Sisler's book of readings for Ag Ec 100Sample Questions PhD Exam1968-1989413Ag Ec 717 - 83:1 Course Outline1983414
Scope and Contents
Research Methods in Ag Economics.
Processing Information
All of the following Ag 717 folders were taped together originally.Ag Ec 717 - 83:2 List of Important References and Topics1983415Ag Ec 717 - 83:3 Exercise 11983416Ag Ec 717 - 83:4 Exercise 31983417Ag Ec 717 - 83:5 What is Science?1983418Ag EC 717 - 83:6 Definitions of Experiment1983419Ag Ec 717 - 83:7 Excerpts from Ferber and Verdorn1983420Ag Ec 717 - 83:8 The All Too Human Enterprise Called Science.1983421Ag Ec 717 - Philosophy and Scientific Methods1983422Ag Ec 717 -83:9 Some Important Concepts1983423Ag Ec 717 - 83:10 Analyzing an Argument1983424Ag Ec 717 - 83:12 Abstract from MS Thesis in Ag Econ1983425Ag Ec 717 - AJAE Abstracts1983426
Scope and Contents
"Award winning theses"Ag Ec 717 - Basic Economic Concepts and their Measurement1983427Ag Ec 717 - 83:14 Exercise 1 Examples1983428Ag Ec 717 Problem Set #21983429Ag Ec 717 Sampling Concepts and Methods1983430Ag Ec 717 Impacts of Investment1983431Ag Ec 717 Simulation1983432Ag Ec 717 Tomek AJAE Article, Waugh Article1983433AdvisingCarlos Roja, Advisee1988217
Scope and Contents
Includes correspondence before and during Mr Rojas time at Cornell under Prof Sisler.Faculty Advising Handbook1994-1995218

Scope and Contents
Includes dept. correspondence about Prof Sisler's advisee, plus PhD of Mr. Ashraf. Note on folder says: "First graduate student Dr. Sisler had!"Jan Low (1)1985-1989224
Scope and Contents
Includes correspondence between Prof Sisler and Jan Low, dept. info regarding PhD status. Note on folder says: "Typical Sisler PhD".Jan Low (2)1990-1994225

Includes correspondence between Prof Sisler and Jan Low, dept. info regarding PhD status. Note on folder says: "Typical Sisler PhD".Untitled Cassette TapeTR-159161987-11-02225AcknowledgementsUndated">226

Includes review of reward system for teachers; several reports on instruction - evaluation, recognition, professionalism.PublicationsAuthored by SislerPublications (1)1959-198441

Includes completed manuscript of research paper.The Whole Food Situation - What is the US Role?197844

Includes completed manuscript and graphs.Rubber or Rice? The Dilemma of Many Developing Nations198045

Includes completed article manuscript. Co-authored with David Blandford.Alternative Sources of Economic Growth196446

Includes manuscript for symposium paper. Co-authored with Barclay G. Jones.Thesis: Direct Production Payments in the Feed-Grain-Livestock Sector of American Agriculture19624

Bound thesis.CIIFAD (Cornell Institute for International Food and Agricultural Development)Expenses 1989-90 CIIFAD1988-1990410

Includes trip expenses and correspondence for CIIFAD.CIIFAD Background Papers1989-1992411
Scope and Contents
Includes proposal to establish and fund CIIFAD, draft of mission statement, related research and correspondence about seminars.CIIFAD Uganda Trip1989412
Scope and Contents
Includes travel preparations, correspondence, program proposals and trip report for Uganda trip.Awards and memorabiliaPhysical awardsFriends of Cornell HockeyMU-1869Undated3

Wooden plaque: "Dan and Carol Sisler Pacesetter".Purdue University honorary doctorateMU-18701989-12-173
Scope and Contents
Fold out presentation case, one side describing Dr. Sisler's achievements, the other containing honorary Doctor of Agriculture.1993 Northeast Regional AwardMU-187119933

Award mounted in glass and wood.Professor of the Year AwardMU-18731982-07-133

Certificate, Cornell, awarded by Council for Advancement and Support of Education.
